desc:
This section of the Old Forest Road runs in a straight line from east to west.
Although it was built with great skill, centuries of use have not been kind, and
the road has become rough and unkempt. The woods on either side encroach upon
the road, with dark trunks rising just feet from the road itself, looming
inwards and all but meeting above almost as if Mirkwood is poised to strike and
swallow the road in one rustling gulp.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road continues east and west, lying upon a bed of solid rock apparently
carved out of the short bluff to the north. The trees and branches to the south
lean out above the road, which meet with the branches of the trees towards the
north. The dense canopy makes it very difficult to see the sky at all. To the
south of the road, the ground slopes downwards and is quickly obscured behind
large bushes covered in thick, sharp and black thorns.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The stonework of the road is worn and weathered. The paving is cracked and in
places grass and small plants sprout up through the road's surface. The forest
to the south looks oppressive, and no visible path weaves between trunk, root,
or shrub. The tree-tops rise a little higher to the north-west, though it is
hardly noticeable through the canopy.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road continues here, east and west, a solitary straight line amidst the
dark, foreboding forest. On the edge of the road a stone rises from the forest
floor. The stone is covered by fallen leaves and lichen. Branches span the road
overhead, but appear to be thinning to the east. To the north an exceptionally
thick hedge of thistle and thorn-covered bushes bars the way.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The forest is held back by the Old Forest Road here. Instead of the sprawling
crowns of trees, a narrow shred of the open sky is visible above. The road
continues straight on to the east and west, carving a path through Mirkwood.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The ancient forest road is largely overgrown by bushes and shrubs; the trees of
Mirkwood stands further away from the road, as if held at bay by the thorny
brush. In some sections of the road, where large bushes have threatened to block
it completely, someone has taken a blade to them. Thus atleast a narrow path to
follow is ensured.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The ancient forest road continues on through the depths of Mirkwood to the east
and west. The brooding trees stand some distance from the road here, as if
hesitant to approach it. Instead, the terrain is dominated by large, sprawling
bushes. The road continues through brush to the west, while the forest gathers
courage to the east and the road disappears beneath the shrouds of a dense
canopy.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The worn roadway makes a wide turn as it continues beneath the dark, stifling
wood. Massive boughs reach out from every trunk, grating against and fusing with
neighbouring trees to form a natural system of cramped tunnels beneath. The road
itself carries on as the largest of these gnarled and worn tunnels.
name: Obstruction in the Old Forest Road

desc:
Encroaching gnarled oaks grow close enough together to fuse and form numerous
solid walls of creaking wood. A great thickness of foliage in the canopy
combines with dense tree growth to make both darkness and stale air prominent
within the deeps of the wood. No traces of daylight reach the forest floor, and
not one current of air stirs so much as a single leaf on the highest bough.
name: Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
A slope, rugged and forest-clad, ascends into the Emyn-nu-Fuin. It begins as an
imperceptible rise in the forest floor to the west, and becomes increasingly
steep to the east. Rugged, rocky soil covers the twisted feet of colossal oaks
as they break and split their way through thick bedrock below. Several species
of fungi hang from stone faces and fill even the thinnest cracks as dark mosses
grow widespread upon the hard bark of trunk and branch.
name: Wooded Foothills

desc:
Gargantuan trees tower over a wide boulder-strewn mountainside. Exposed slabs of
pure granite create steep escarpments between wide patches of rock-filled soil.
Various swaths of the forest floor reveal broken stone blocks partially masked
beneath layers of great dark roots.
name: Stony Mountainside

desc:
The forest in this area mainly consists of dark elms and lichen-covered oaks.
The lichens sway slightly in the wind, resembling the long grey beards of
dwarven-folk. The ground is covered with twisted roots and sickly green
undergrowth. A flat broken slab of stone is barely visible beneath the foliage.
name: Foreboding Forest

desc:
The forest here is quite dense, and there are many fallen trees that make
travelling here troublesome. The fallen trees are mostly rotten, and they are
covered by fungi and lichen. Even the thicker trunks scattered about the forest
floor break apart at the slightest touch.
name: Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
This part of forest is almost impassable; thickets of vines, intertwined roots
and branches make walking here tiresome. To the north, it looks like the forest
is completely overgrown, creating a wall of interwined vegetation. For a good
climber it would perhaps be easier to move above near the treetops if one could
climb up there.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
A very thick and tall black yew is growing in the area. Its branches thicker
than a leg, its bark tough and black as night, seems to control the area with
its strong presence. Locked in a never-ending battle with vines, roots, and the
forest itself, the black yew manages to hold all other vegetation at bay as it
stands here in lone majesty. It looks like one could climb up the tree along its
strong branches.
name: Beneath a Sprawling Black Yew

desc:
An almost imperceptible path is here, running in a westward direction. It parts
the trees on either side of it, and no grass grows on it. A thin layer of dirt
lies upon this ancient foot path. Small shrubs grow on either side and
occasionally spill over into it. To the south, a seemingly impassable wall of
vegetation blocks the way.
name: Ancient Forest

desc:
Several trees bear the scars of axes and many more have been badly burnt. The
trees seem to have sustained these injuries many years ago as part of an epic
battle fought through the woods. The forest here is a bit sparser than the
surrounding forest, which makes travelling here relatively easy.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
This part of the forest died a long time ago and only a few blackened, rotten
stumps remain. Fallen boughs of once-mighty beech trees lie rotting upon the
ground, mosses and lichens having engulfed them in a blanket of green and
yellow. The damp air is thick with the scent of decay, and despite there being
fewer trees here, it still feels as foreboding as the darker areas of Mirkwood.
The forest descends suddenly to the west.
name: Dead Forest

desc:
No sign of the sky can be seen overhead as the trees crowd in close and the
foliage forms a dense canopy above. So closely do they grow that it is almost
impossible to find a path through them. The ground is full of places where roots
have broken through, and care should be taken when putting down a foot or
guiding a mount through. Fallen branches lie scattered about on the forest
floor, covered in mould and grey moss.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
An enormous tree stump lies here emitting a strong, rotten smell from its core.
The smell is that of long-dead flesh but the stump looks like ordinary wood that
is long dead, sodden, and rotted through. An almost tangible veil of darkness
seem to hang from the trees.
name: At a Foetid Tree Stump

desc:
The trees in this part of Mirkwood are dark and foreboding. They rise on
gnarled, twisted trunks that seem to bend and buckle under the weight of
massive, sprawling crowns. The thick foliage above forms an impenetrable ceiling
that blots out the sky with more finality than even the darkness of Sauron could
muster.
name: Under a Dark Forest Canopy

desc:
Within this patch of forest, shrubs of all kinds grow. The ground is moist and
soft and very fertile, serving as excellent soil for plants and trees to grow
in. Tall pine trees, their branches reaching down as if to grab at something,
creak under the weight of their crowns. Their needles are very sharp and almost
seem to gleam threatingly. The shifting trees give the sense that nothing can
move here without being seen by their vigilant gaze.
name: Malevolent Pines

desc:
What were once great oaks, have now become twisted and warped trunks covered in
foliage that runs in shades of black and brown. It seems almost as if the
massive quantity of ivy and lichen that grow on them drains their life away. The
forest floor here is covered in layers of rotting leaves that fill the air with
a faint scent of decay. Dry twigs and branches can be seen poking up through the
layer of leaves in places.
name: Among Withering Oaks

desc:
The forest floor rises upwards slowly towards the east where a mighty tree is
visible. The trees grow thick and tall on the slope to the south; ash and oak
stand among dense groves of oddly twisted pines. The forest floor is moist and
the air clear despite hanging vines dangling like delicate webs from overhead
branches. The only evidence of activity here are small animal tracks in the
undergrowth.
name: Forested Incline

desc:
While the dense foliage makes it difficult to see, something seems to hang up
there, suspended high in the trees. There are no easy places to climb up the
looming trees though. The undergrowth shows signs of being trampled, as if this
area sees traffic from large creatures with some regularity. Thick, thorny
bushes growing between the trees prevent passage to the north and south.
name: Shadowy Forest

desc:
The ground, where it's not overgrown by thorny bushes and thistles, is littered
with sharp rocks. High overhead, just beneath the thick canopy, cocoons and webs
of silky threads sway gently in the wind. Tall, gnarled linden trees appear to
twist and shake in even the slightest breeze sending a rustling sound through
the woods.
name: Deep in Mirkwood Forest

desc:
An unusual variety of trees grow here; ash, oak, fir, elm and alder. Their
branches intertwine high in the canopy where they form great twisted arches of
branch and leaf. The entwined branches from a giant dome-like structure in hues
of green and brown beneath which a tainted crowd of thistles and thorns gather
amid the trunks and roots. The foliage is less dense to the south where a road
is barely visible through branches and shrubs.
name: Under Some Arched Trees

desc:
The ground between the trees is dotted with black flowers that spread their
petals to catch the feeble rays of light that break through the canopy above.
Even the few small flowers that have managed to grow here give the place a
vibrancy that is missing in the darker areas of Mirkwood.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
The trees in this part of Mirkwood grow so dense that the bark of the gnarled
trees rub and scrape against one another. Great, wide branches spread out above,
creating a thick canopy that leave only hints of a sky visible beyond.
name: Menacing Forest

desc:
Great gnarled oaks grow closely together to form mishapen tunnels beneath their
many fused branches. Long strands of lichen dangle from the upper boughs,
standing still in the stuffy stale air under the ancient trees that serve to
block both wind and breeze from reaching the lower reaches of the forest.
name: Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
The wooded foothills dip down into a bowl-shaped depression, making the already
stale air even denser and therefore thicker. Untamed foliage encroaches upon
even the lowest tree branches while serving to block out any light that might
have reached the foresaken forest floor. Massive clumps of thick, spore-covered
fungi permeate all of the earth not occupied by hulking trunks covered in shed
spores and rotting moss.
name: Dark Depression

desc:
Great hulking trees with warped boughs thrust threateningly from the empty
ground here. Dry, cool earth has formed a thin crust above a frenzy of tangled
roots laying just below the surface, evidenced by their sources standing tall
and menacing. The air is quiet and still, save the occasional creak and
infrequent rustling among the leaves.
name: Desolate Woods

desc:
The trees grow so close to one another that their branches intertwine, forming
an impenetrable barrier to the north and west. Ancient trunks with rough,
cracking bark creak to and fro as they sway in some unheard and unfelt wind. The
sheer volume of undergrowth choking the forest floor belies what little light
manages to pierce the upper canopy during the day. Large beds of ancient moss,
partially covered by thin layers of rotting fir needles, lie beneath thick
tangles of vines that grip both trunk and stone.
name: Densely-packed Firs

desc:
Old, gnarled trees twist and turn toward one another as they reach around large
boulders laying strewn throughout the woods. A near-vertical cliff juts up out
of the soil to the north, covered almost entirely by a thick crust of green
lichen. Massive roots dominate the ground not occupied by hulking stone as they
continue to ruthlessly split rock and puncture soil on their relentless pursuit
of that which is nowhere to be found above ground.
name: Steep Forested Hillside

desc:
Massive fir trees growing in great clumps have created a vast but very dark
grove inside the forest of Mirkwood. Their towering height, combined with their
large number of boughs and branches block nearly all light from reaching the
ground. Great gnarled roots plunge into the earth around the bases of the trees,
leaving paths between them covered in the shed needles of those malevolent and
ancient entities.
name: Within a Gloomy Grove

desc:
Thick, knobby trunks intrude upon one another's space as branches intermingle
and boughs grow together within the depths of Mirkwood. Clumps of matted ivy
race across the ground to claim all soil not already occupied by the great
trees, which stand tall and foreboding in spite of their twisted forms.
name: Deep in a Dreary Forest

desc:
The thick, strong boughs in the lower reaches of the trees grow together so
tightly and so completely that they form an artificial floor in the canopy,
preventing access to the ground altogether. Upon the natural wooden platform
rest thick piles of dead and dying leaves, decomposing to form the homes of
strange fungi that sprout up in various clusters.
name: Atop Tangled Boughs

desc:
Crowded trees, steep earthy walls and countless tangling vines combine to form a
stifling ravine. The ground is covered with tough, springy creeper growth in
every direction reaching up above the top of the ravine itself. Even the tree
roots themselves are overrun by one senseless mass of interwoven runners.
name: Vine-choked Ravine

desc:
Columns of great oaks and firs reach to the unseen skies along this curved,
steep slope. Titanic stones in the form of sheer rock walls prevent passage
north and east. Mighty roots thrust downward from the trees themselves, cracking
and splitting stone even as great boulders from above lay resting atop broken
and shattered trunks in an age-old battle of tree versus mountain.
name: Wooded Escarpment

desc:
Two exceptionally large pine trees grow here amidst a dark forest of oak and
beech. A faint smell of decay comes from the ground where the lower layers of
needles and leaves rot beneath more recent fallen foliage. A gate of wood,
reinforced with bands of metal, is standing to the west. A high stockade
encloses the parts of the area, making it impossible to see what is inside.
Above the gate, the huge, fanged head of a spider is nailed onto a beam.
name: At a Wooden Gate

desc:
A rugged mountain slope rises above the lower forest to the south. It is not
steep but the terrain is scraggy and strenuous to travel across. The forest
grows thick and strong in spite of the difficult terrain. Round,
lichen-encrusted stones litter the bases of trees and form strange piles on beds
of dark green moss wherever the trees appear slightly less dense.
name: Rugged Slope

desc:
A rugged slope rises above Mirkwood; the foothills of Emyn-nu-Fuin to the
north-east. The slope ends abrutly at an almost uniformally high natural stone
wall. The vertical cliff suddenly shoots upward from among the rocks and tree
trunks without warning, blocking passage to the east. Dark, stringy shrubs have
pushed their way through the topsoil, revealing their small but menacing
tentacle-like vines that grasp greedily at all other vegetation nearby.
name: Slope at the Base of a Great Cliff

desc:
Steep foreboding cliffs cuts through the wide, boulder-strewn ridge on its west
side; a wooded slope is visible at their feet. Mighty trees, ancient oaks and
towering firs, grow abundantly in thick groves. Encrusted lichen forms sporadic
patches on the grey boulders that rests under the dark shadows of the foliage.
name: Boulder-strewn Ridge

desc:
Wickedly sharp splintered stones jut from the grey, rocky surface of this wide
ridge. Ancient fir and oak trees grow among the rubble, forming a dense canopy
overhead. Strands of dangling lichen hang from nearly every branch and boulder.
Old moss spreads out cross the ridge, entirely filling the various cracks and
fissures produced by the insatiable growth of swelling roots.
name: Rugged Ridge

desc:
Even large trees have somehow managed to grow in the nearly vertical walls of
this narrow gorge. The gorge leads upward to the south, snaking this way and
that. The steep mountainsides to the east block passage higher into the
Emyn-nu-Fuin. Long, tensile vines stretch from branch to rock face and back
again as they wind their way up jagged cracks and through tangled rootballs.
Devoid of any water, the gorge floor consists solely of smooth but
frequently-cracked stone, various large trees bursting through fractured piles
of pebbled rubble, and mounds of dead leaves that have yet to decay into a
sparse layer of soil.
name: Steep Gorge

desc:
This wide, rugged ridge, gloomy and perilous, is but a harbinger of the higher
mountains to the north-east. Thin slices of sharp shale form frequent
outcroppings beneath heavily knotted oaks that seem to meld together as their
twisted roots and stumpy branches intertwine. The stones themselves thrust
upward from unknown depths, lacerating the surface soil to protrude dangerously
between great clumps of tangled shrubbery.
name: Shale Outcroppings under Oaks

desc:
Untold numbers of towering firs crowd the uneven top and steep sides of a wide,
rugged ridge stretching from north to south. Large boulders lay strewn between
many of the ancient trunks as they share one continuous bed of twisting tangled
roots. Atop boulder, root and trunk alike grows a constant bed of dark green
moss that fills every cranny and crevice.
name: Wide Forested Ridge

desc:
These bluffs overlook a vast, green blanket of canopy to the west. The steep
cliffs rise atleast a hundred feet to get to these plateu-like bluffs, and a
broad-faced stone wall rising to the north towers another fifty feet. The faces
of the stonewalls are covered in grey-green moss and white lichen. Trees crowd
on these rugged mountains, chiefly mammoth oaks and towering firs. Around the
base of every trunk grows large clumps of dark green fungi with fine hairs
protruding from fat stalks topped with bell-shaped caps.
name: Bluff beneath the Trees

desc:
Untold numbers of jumbled boulders have come to rest in the remnants of a great
rockslide on these rugged mountains. The rockslide seems to have originated from
the north, where the mountains climb yet higher still. Tall trees have long
since grown over the entire slide, once again filling the forest with darkness
and stifling the winds that once wafted down through a rare and brief gap in the
trees. A thick layer of light-green lichen covers every surface of each
individual stone and drapes lazily from various rocky overhangs in thick
curtains.
name: Overgrown Rockslide

desc:
No light reaches the depths of this narrow stone-walled canyon, and no air
currents stir at its bottom. The canyon continues to the north, squeezed in
between the ridges and crags of Emyn-nu-Fuin. It's filled to the brim with tall
firs warring for the relatively small amount of space available along the
cracked stone floor, all other vegetation has been choked out by the thick
straining roots of the towering trees themselves.
name: Steep Canyon in Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
This canyon is surrounded on three sides by steep mountainsides that looms
threateningly, as if ready to bury this narrow canyon beneath the mountains at
any moment. Looming fir trees grow so densely here that they threaten to become
one great mass of tangled gnarled wood with only low passages still present
about their feet. Large knots appear frequently upon the thick trunks like boils
on the necks of trolls while long, abundant boughs interleave such that it is
difficult to distinguish one trunk from the next.
name: End of a Tree-choked Canyon

desc:
Mountainous slopes climb towards the heights of Emyn-nu-Fuin. The terrain is
intersected by ravines, crags, and jutting outcroppings of dark, moss-clad
granite. Yet Mirkwood prevails even here, as trees, bushes, and undervegetation
thrive. The trees are mostly gnarled oaks and ancient firs; they spread their
great boughs overhead, and create a green roof that shrouds all but the largest
outcroppings. Winding masses of loose vines hang ominously from stony crags and
narrow ledges, forming black tangles that seem to be in constant movement.
name: Dark, Rugged Slopes

desc:
While mountains here on the southern outskirts of Emyn-nu-Fuin are not towering,
looming peaks, they still make formidable obstacles for a traveller. These
slopes are rugged, covered in broken rock and treacherous crevices. Mirkwood,
ancient and brooding, covers these parts of the mountains completely, enveloping
them in blankets of brown and green. Broad, gnarled roots thrust downard into
the dark soil. Hulking arms of hard, twisting wood have splintered the rock
beneath the topsoil to produce an uneven slope littered with jagged granite
shards precariously covered by a thin layer of rotting leaves.
name: Dark, Jagged Slopes

desc:
A bald dome bulges from the surrounding forest. Its sides are rounded, worn
smooth from centuries of erosion. Rough, mottled lichen coats the surface in an
uneven layer. Aged linden trees crowd at the base of the dome, gnarled branches
crawling up the sides as if to drag the mountain down.
name: Weathered Summit

desc:
A narrow range of the Mirkwood Mountains rises in a double peak of jagged
summits separated by a shallow dip. This hollow is covered in a tangle of
crooked trees growing around massive boulders. The summits themselves are
covered in black, twisted shrubs growing in rocky soil.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The range of mountains dips to form a narrow valley surrounded on three sides by
densely forested slopes. Overhanging branches spill over into the hollow,
tangling with the crowns of the trees that grow from the depths of the valley.
Black, sinuous creepers cling to branch, trunk, and twig. Their sharp thorns
catch on passing creatures; movement only serves to cause further entanglement.
name: Snarled Hollow

desc:
Piles of rocks and boulders mix with tangled and knobby roots, making for rough
terrain that is strenuous to traverse. Dense shrubbery thickened by fallen
leaves hide perilous, narrow crevices that cut deeply into the slopes. Eerie
chittering emanates from these gaps, hinting at creatures that thrive in
darkness deeper than that under the shadows of the trees.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
Strange rock spires crown a string of knobbed crags. Their tips gaze over the
treetops. Their bases are covered in phosphorescent moss making them appear to
glow at any time of the day. These luminous pillars contrast starkly with the
surrounding murk; enveloping branches cast eerie, skeletal shadows.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
Great cracks stretch across these hills. The larger cracks broaden into crevices
several yards wide. Fallen trunks and thick, gnarled roots form natural bridges
across these gaps. Vines trail from the trees and wind around blackened
vegetation that creeps in from the murky shadows below.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
An unbroken wall of densely packed oaks runs along the north side of an uneven,
rocky slope. These thick, broad-branched trees crowd out all other vegetation,
and stand in stark contrast to snarled undergrowth that forms a writhing mesh
down the slope to the south. These two extremes are held separate by a rocky
slope between them, where the trees are more sparse, but the sterile soil
prevents the growth of underbrush.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
Giant, hulking oaks tower over uneven terrain. Swelling hills stretching for
miles are visible between the widely-spaced tree trunks. Misshapen mushrooms
with caps several feed wide cover the spaces between trees. These fungi rest in
a bed of putridly decaying foliage fallen from the canopy.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The slanted ground beneath long, slender trees in the darkness of the forested
hillside is covered entirely in soft green moss. The light-coloured vegetation
blankets every stone and engulfs every trunk, even growing between exposed roots
and down into cracks in the hard gnarled wood itself.
name: Moss-covered Forest Hillside

desc:
The forest floor rises steeply to the east with many twisted roots weaving over
one another amidst large rotten branches that lay scattered about the darkness.
Exposed rocks dot the slope, resting firmly in hard packed soil that appears
resistant to smaller vegetation beneath the mighty trees that devour all light
from above.
name: Dense Forested Incline

desc:
Trees and shrubs are indistinguishable as they suffocate one another in an
engulfing blur of darkness beneath the impenetrable canopy. The forest ground
seems to rise toward the north and decline to the west, covered in sharp
thickets and thorny bushes with wicked spines protruding from trailing creepers.
name: Forest on a Mountain Slope

desc:
An east-west line of mountains dips as a valley cuts into it from the north.
Grey, crooked lindens cling to the slopes, converging with gnarled oaks that
march in from the valley. Twisted roots covered in slimy moss alternate with
lichen-blanketed rock to provide slick footing on the slopes.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
A forest-covered ridgeline runs east-to-west, punctuated by needle-like peaks.
The hollows between peaks are covered densely in lindens; their low-hanging
branches drag at any creature that passes between them. The summits themselves
are covered in broken slabs blanketed in grey-green lichen. The heights afford a
view of a twisted promontory that rises across a narrow valley to the north.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The walls of this cave are riven with numerous cracks which allow narrow shreds
of light to pass into the chamber. Large quartz crystals reflect this light,
casting shimmering motes on the walls. Wind whispers through the cracks,
muffling any sound in the cave, be it footfalls or predatory paws.
name: Whispering Grotto

desc:
A narrow, twisting cavern descends from west to east. Thin streams of wind issue
from small openings in the walls and ceiling; none of these holes are more than
a hand's-breadth wide. The sound of these streams is soft but persistent, a
whispering murmur that fills the air.
name: Whispering Grotto

desc:
Broad tunnels, steep rock chimneys, and deep chutes meet at a great cavern
junction. The ceiling of the cavern is largely lost in the murk, from which
great pillars of rock reach down to touch the floor. The massive size of the
cavern, and its rough, irregular walls mean that even the slightest sound makes
a distorted echo that thwarts any attempt to discern its origin.
name: Whispering Grotto

desc:
A broad round cavern splits into a plethora of small tunnels, most of which are
less than a foot wide, and all are too narrow to enter. Many of these tunnels
carry distant echoes of unidentifiable origin. The rocks are mostly black and
unreflective, though a few crystals scattered throught the cavern might reflect
fleeting motes of lantern or torch.
name: Whispering Grotto

desc:
A long but narrow ledge snakes high on the cavern wall. Here and there the ledge
runs behind towering rock pillars, while in other places there is nothing to
stop one from falling off the ledge to smash into the cavern's floor several
yards below the ledge. A constant breeze flows through the ledge, filling the
air with its soft susurration.
name: Ledge above a Large Cavern

desc:
A twisted mesh of thistle, shrub, and branch resists any attempt to shove it
aside. Disturbed foliage snaps back quickly when released, entrapping creatures
that trespass in this domain. Heavy, low-hanging branches bar the narrow gaps
between trees. A steep hill to the north looms over the snarled tangle of trees
and vines.
name: Entangled Trees

desc:
Squat, gnarled oaks pack together closely in this rounded dip in the valley.
Branches reach out from the trees as if to grasp at any creature foolish enough
to pass nearby. Webs dangle from the outstretched twigs: snares ready to catch
hapless creatures. Simply put, this place is a trap.
name: Entangled Trees

desc:
This deep cavern exists below the trees of Mirkwood. The opening at the surface
admits no light, as tangled foliage and loose-looking rocks hide this rift from
any birds-eye view. The walls of the round cave are rough and uneven, while the
floor is dotted by medium-sized rocks. Faint intermittent chittering echoes from
the walls, distorted by numerous cracks and crannies.
name: Tenebrous Cavern

desc:
Ragged briars spread across the ground, threaded by thorny black vines. Thick,
sticky webs stretch between branches; fallen limbs drag strands of silk with
them, entangling them with the underbrush. This dense morass causes only the
lower boles of the trees are visible. These bear gaping cracks which bleed foul
sap that oozes along the creepers, coating them in sticky residue.
name: Entangled Trees

desc:
Thick roots snake across broken terrain, shadowed by overlapping boughs in the
canopy high overhead. Swishing veils of vines drape from the limbs, accumulating
in tangled piles at the bases of the trees. Branches and leaves appear to float,
stuck in the silken webs that stretch between trunks. This fallen foliage is
joined by husks of long dead creatures.
name: Entangled Trees

desc:
Large black oaks with dark leaves cover the forest floor in an eternal darkness.
Thick moss clings to the great tree trunks, while wiry lichen dangles from
oversized knotted boughs. The air is thick and heavy beneath the ominous canopy
looming above.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Gnarled oaks and towering firs stand strong amidst a sea of twisting roots and
knotted vines. Thick, hard roots thrust violently in and out of the soft soil in
every direction beneath a tangled web of stiff, raspy vine runners attempting to
strangle the life from every living thing in the forest.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Beneath the overarching trunks and limbs that block out all natural light from
above lies a dark, moist soil that serves as a guarded home for all manner of
fungus. The sheltered bed of warm moisture harbours large black foul-smelling
fungi, putrid green mushrooms, and bright red bells popping up all along the
forest floor.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Inside a great circle of towering firs covered by an impenetrable canopy of
thick boughs and intertwined branches lies a dark silent grove decorated by
every manner of lichen. Large crusts of yellow lichen cling to the surrounding
tree trunks while leafy, light green lichen grows along exposed roots in the
soil itself. Thin, shrub-like grey lichen dangles from every branch fading up
into the darkness of the canopy.
name: Lichen-strung Grove

desc:
The trees grow so thick here that, at the height of the midday sun, even a faint
dim green glow is absent beneath the impervious canopy. Thorny vines, tall
bushes and entangling roots cover the forest floor. Rotting black logs lay
scattered along the ground, brought down by some unknown force long ago under
the cover of complete darkness. Bones of various creatures litter the ground,
including one obvious humanoid skeleton.
name: Black Forest

desc:
The trees grow so closely together here that their branches and boughs
intertwine to form a dense lattice of gnarled wood just above the ground. Thick
roots twisting in and out of one another make up the forest floor, covering up
whatever soil might lie beneath the hard warped feet of each grizzled tree
trunk.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Immense boughs tower ominously high above the ground from mighty trunks that
loom upward in the silent darkness of the forest. The air is still and stuffy as
no breeze will penetrate the mighty canopy of Mirkwood. Dry dead leaves lay
thick between moss-covered stones that litter the forest floor.
name: Shadowy Forest

desc:
The irregular knobby branches of tall gnarled trees interweave to create an
ominous roof, blotting out the sky above and shrouding the forest floor in
unending darkness. Slithering vines have lashed out in all directions,
strangling shrubs and choking trunks as they feast off of the life-blood of
other vegetation along the ground.
name: Ascending Forest

desc:
The dark earth beneath a fallen tree has been dug out to form a sizeable den for
what must be a very large beast. The floor consists of compacted soil covered
with colossal dusty paw prints and a few scattered coarse hairs.
name: Sheltered Den

desc:
Dark oaks and withered pine trees grow at awkward angles as the forest floor
rises dramatically in elevation from the south. Long roots grow shallow under
the soil, uprooting loose rocks in a scattered pattern throughout the terrain.
Dark and slimy umbrella mushrooms cling to the surfaces of the rocks, exuding a
noxious odour.
name: Steep Overgrown Mountainside

desc:
Beneath the untold thickness of the forest canopy lie warped tree trunks looming
in the pitch-black air, thick with dust and malice. Gigantic fungi grow on
rotten tree stumps between thorny bushes that cover giant boulders. Moss grows
thick on every stone surface, while strands of strange lichen dangle from nearly
every bough along the steep and rugged mountains.
name: Warped Wooded Mountainside

desc:
Nestled in the steep wooded slopes of Mirkwood low in the mountains of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin, a grove of great oaks stand bent by years beyond reckoning. The
mighty forms of these withered trees even in their tired state envelope huge
boulders with their terribly strong roots of enormous girth. Decrepit, knobbed
branches still stretch defiantly outward in every direction, each fully clad in
unusually long drapes of dry wiry lichen.
name: Ancient Withered Mountain Grove

desc:
Masses of dark fir trees grow in twisted shapes upon a steep slope in the
shadowy forest. The bark of the trees appears to hang from the strangely-shaped
boughs like willowy drapes, while dark vines dangle in the stifling air from
various branches high in the canopy. Slime mould covers various boulders that
dot the mountainous forest floor even as bracken struggles to survive in the
unending darkness.
name: Steep Fir-covered Slope

desc:
A narrow, winding pass has formed between steep cliffs and unforgiving mountain
slopes. The pass winds its way through unstable scree and along steep gullies
plunging into darkness eternal. The pass continues, in some places under looming
overhangs of dark grey stone, and in other places it narrows considerably due to
the huge trees that grow here.
name: Precarious Mountain Pass

desc:
Beneath the shade of a few dark trees, a pass winds its way through the
mountains, weaving around great crags and atop uneven precipices. Slick round
stones dot the precarious slopes amidst long patches of dried stiff thickets
harbouring long, hollow thorns ending in needle-like points. Darkness covers all
as the unrelenting forest canopy blocks out light and fresh air both.
name: Winding Mountain Pass

desc:
A narrow mountain pass weaves between steep heights and wanders seemingly
without end. Precarious cliffs rise far above to the north, harboring naught but
desolation in their weathered crags and wicked cracks. In spite of the rugged
cliffside no light reaches this stoney place, as masses of great trees crowd out
the light and air from above as they greedily envelop the skies in a great
curtain of darkness all the way up to and against the cliffside.
name: Winding Mountain Pass

desc:
A winding pass in the mountains cuts a serpentine scar through the rugged
terrain, littered with boulders big and small. A tall crest ascends to the north
with no sign of life upon it, jutting from the brutal foliage that devours all
light and smothers all else. The heights close in, masking the horizon with
their jagged outlines.
name: Winding Mountain Pass

desc:
Steep inclines on gravel-covered bedrock mark a seldom-used trail leading
through the lower Mountains of Mirkwood. An unfelt wind whistles through the
unseen treetops above, even as the air remains still and thick beneath the
stifling girth of the canopy looming over the trail itself. Piles of splintered
stone surround large, cracked boulders that provide homes for small stiff bushes
that have taken root between the sharp edges of the cracks themselves.
name: Rugged Mountain Pass

desc:
Two moderately high peaks rise up through dark baleful woods beneath the choking
canopy of the smothering forest. One peak to the northwest thrusts itself up
through a stand of dark, withered oaks while another cracked stone peak rises up
to the southeast amidst a grove of bent pines. The slopes of the peaks are lined
with sharp crags and deep recesses that slice the bark of those trees that grow
close enough to rub against their mighty walls in unseen winds high above.
name: Mountain Pass beneath Two Peaks

desc:
Crags of jagged stone rise from the ground like crooked teeth, forming a winding
path between the rocks that rises steadily to the north. Dark trees stand tall
above the crooked stone still blocking out light as well as wind even on the
heights of the low Mountains of Mirkwood.
name: Rising Mountain Pass

desc:
The entangled vegetation shrouds the lower forest canopy into unending darkness.
Tough, clinging vines and sticky webs conspire to make a tangled snarl, limiting
the view in many places to a few yards. The poor vision is further restricted by
protruding branches, entangling creepers, and the endless amount of thick silk
strands that stretch between the trees.
name: Entangled Lower Canopy

desc:
A round narrow tunnel woven from spider silk, smaller branches, and sticky
elastic mass cuts through the dark vegetation. There are occasional openings in
the walls of the tunnel, providing small side paths that soon disappear into
murk. The tunnel makes a tight curve leading towards the north and east,
elevating slightly up towards the north. The floor is sticky, making the
travelling slow.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
A circular, sticky tunnel zig-zags within the canopy. It takes it course where
the branches are providing support, flanks around the dark tree trunks, and in
general arches to the south and east. Rotting vines and shattered branches
litter the floor of the tunnel. The tunnel walls themselves have been created
from grey spider silk.
name: Tenebrous, Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Sticky webs form thick nets that drape the canopy of densely tangled trees. The
silken strands sway ominously with the motion of the trees, accompanied by
harmonic vibrations produced by the creatures who skitter along these webs. The
branches and silky densely-woven webs form a slender platform which is snaking
between the trees. The surroundings are covered in varying patches of white and
black, a mixture of fallen silk and sticky ooze.
name: Entangled Canopy

desc:
Tall, broad oak trees rise more than a hundred feet to blot out the sky with
their entangled boughs. A few stray beams of sun or moonlight sometimes pierce
into the canopy, revealing sticky strands of silk. These strands are only a
small part of a vast, unbroken web. This spider domain extends in all
directions, the only limit being its height which can reach only to the tops of
the trees.
name: Entangled Canopy

desc:
The entangled branches form narrow platforms that serve as perilous pathways
within the canopy. Here, the thoroughfare encircles a gap in the lower
vegetation allowing a view below to a small, circular forest glade. Above the
clearing, strands of webs stretch in the air in numerous layers. The canopy
itself towers more than one hundred feet above the forest floor. Cocoons and egg
sacks dangle from the webs.
name: Within the Canopy

desc:
A heavy mat of creepers spreads over this broad, forested valley. These creepers
are blackened and rotting, yet still summon the strength to battle the oak trees
that grow here. An ominous rustle of vine against root fills the air. The canopy
of the oak trees eclipses the sky; not even during a bright summer day could
light penetrate this dense obscuring foliage.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Dense foliage limits visibility to little more than a few yards. Paths open
suddenly among the shifting trees, only to close moments later. Vines swing
freely across these ephemeral passages, drawing attention to their fleeting
presence. Though animal life is sparse, a quiet murmur pervades these woods.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
An overwhelming stench of rot permeates the air. The oaks in this dense forest
ooze jet-black sap, and many have fallen, leaving behind twisted stumps. The
density of the forest prevents the collapsed trees from reaching the forest
floor. Ochre fungus covers the decaying trunks, issuing dense clouds of spores.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
A vast legion of oaks leans into a steep slope, hoary and knobbed trunks bending
up the incline. Their branches hang shackled by thick, draping vines that drag
them towards the valley floor. Twisted roots grasp desperately at the incline as
they strain against these living chains.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Forested highlands form the northern slope of a broad valley which stretches
away to the south and west. Undergrowth clusters densely around the trunks of
aged oaks, as if seeking to strangle these hoary trees. Many of the gnarled
branches lack foliage, and the swell of the southern flank of the valley is
visible through these wide gaps.
name: Tangled Ravine

desc:
A deep ravine cuts through the forested highlands. Here at its eastern end the
ravine is blocked by towering cliffs to the east and south, while a slope leads
up to a ridge to the north. Rocks and boulders lie scattered at its bottom,
interspersed with dense shrubbery. Branches from trees above cast murky shadows
over the thick underbrush. An unseen stream murmurs quietly, the only natural
sound breaking the silence.
name: Tangled Ravine

desc:
A tall peak looms to the north, its steep face scarred with gaping recesses. The
oaks in its shadow are squat and stooped, as if cowering in its presence. They
stretch horizontally instead: their tangled clumps of roots smother the forest
floor. Sickly tufts of grass peek through the gaps, clinging to life despite the
stranglehold of the trees.
name: Tangled Forest beneath a Peak

desc:
Tall cliffs bound the western edge of this small valley. Decrepit, skeletal
trees fill a hollow at the end of the valley. Brittle branches blanket the
ground, interspersed with brown, dry grass and a layer of leaves and pine
needles.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
A towering peak rises to the north, looming above Mirkwood. Trees, stunted and
twisted, grown among the recesses and boulders of the rugged mountain. A dark
cavern lies half-hidden in one of the shadowy ravines that scars the surface of
the slope.
name: Southern Slope of a Peak

desc:
Mounds of rubble line the floor of a cave in the mountainside. Gaping holes in
the ceiling show where giant slabs broke off to tumble and shatter on the floor
below. Dust blankets every surface, and clouds of it still linger in the air.
name: Crumbling Cavern

desc:
Rugged slopes climb from the south and east, forming the eastern end of a
ridgeline that extends to the west. Spindly firs and stooped oaks cling
desperately to the incline. Their branches tangle mere feet above the ground,
lashing at any passing traveller. Many small rills snake through shallow
crevices down to a ravine in the valley to the south.
name: Ridge above a Ravine

desc:
A narrow valley cuts between towering cliffs. At its western end a fairly
accessible slope leads up to a ridge. The bottom of the cleft is clogged with
dead branches, leaves, and other detritus that gather behind a mound of rubble.
Patches of stumps mar the fir-clad mountainsides.
name: Cleft between Peaks

desc:
Tangled shrubs and stooped firs blanket an otherwise barren and rugged ridge.
The fir branches are spindly and crooked, and seem to grasp at passing
travellers. Patches of scree break the cover of vegetation: the slightest
disturbance sends cascades of rocks tumbling down the slope. Clouds of dust
peppered with gravel fill the air, originating from a mountain to the west.
name: Scraggly Ridge

desc:
Massive slabs protrude from a ridge that extends from a peak to the west. Scree
fills the gaps, making for unstable terrain between secure rocky islands. Clouds
of dust billow from the slopes of the ridge. The scree and rubble is clear
evidence of recent rockfalls.
name: Protruding Ridge

desc:
A steep boulder field forms a comparatively gentle transition between two
parallel cliffs which together descend from a mighty peak lying southwest to a
murky valley to the northeast. The boulders are covered in a thick mat of grey
lichen which makes them very slippery.
name: Battered Boulder Field

desc:
Thick vegetation covers every surface of this rugged slope. Hazardous crevices
lurk in the shade beneath tall trees, hidden by an obscuring carpet of vines and
brambles. A mighty peak towers to the south, its sheer slopes offering no
purchase. The terrain climbs to the east, where a sheer cliff overlooks a valley
to the northeast.
name: Forested Ridge

desc:
Massive, rolling waves of earth run from east to west. Gnarled and bent oaks
cling to the slopes of rising mountains, overshadowing murky hollows between
ridges. Twisted trunks intertwine with thick limbs, leaving little open space.
Beneath these tangles, knobbed roots snake through decaying mounds of leaves.
name: Undulating Forest

desc:
The ground seems to sag as it descends to the east, where jagged hilltops rim a
low, murky valley. Oak trees cling to the slopes, dense webs of roots mixing
with those of their neighbours. Erosion has swept away much of the top layer of
soil, exposing the undersides of the roots where swarms of insects now thrive.
name: Entrance to a Broad Valley

desc:
Giant oaks tower over the western end of a broad valley. Barrel-thick roots
snake through a thick, decaying mat of leaves and acorns, and massive limbs
block out the sky. Tough goutweed proliferates in dense patches between the
trees. An oppressive stench of rot and mould fills the air.
name: Looming Oaks

desc:
Mighty, sprawling oak trees prevail over the southern slope of a wide valley.
Their heavy branches hang low to the ground, tangling with those of neighbours
so that it is often unclear which boughs are attached to which trees. A net of
thick vines drapes from the trees, limiting vision and obscuring the
outstretched limbs.
name: Looming Oaks

desc:
Massive mounds of rock separate two forested valleys. Small shrubs and frail
saplings try to grow in the gaps between boulders, but there is little in the
way of fertile soil. Lichens fare better, draping every exposed face in
orange-brown fuzz.
name: Lichenous Boulder Field

desc:
A sea of oaks swells to the northwest, rising as it meets a steep escarpment.
Jagged rock shelves stretch parallel to the cliff, ominous outcrops along their
edges glaring over the treetops. There are occasional gaps in the shelves where
slabs have broken off, but these breaches are clogged with thick vines and
rotten vegetation.
name: Rugged Forest

desc:
Great mounds of earth rise from the woodlands. Rough boulders stud the slopes,
bracing the steep inclines. Densely packed trees grip the hillsides, their
twisted trunks bent upward as they strain for the sky. Tangled branches drag at
the slopes as if grasping for support.
name: Rugged Hillocks

desc:
A broad ridge stretches westward from a mighty peak to the east. Tall oaks line
these rounded heights, their efforts devoted to supporting high branches. Thick,
tangled vines dangle from the limbs, swaying in rhythm with the winds that shake
the canopy. There is a gap in the tree cover where a flat stone slab covers the
ground. Even there, determined oak roots tear at the rock, sending cracks along
its face.
name: Shadowed Ridge

desc:
This gully is deep enough that it may actually be called a pit, with huge dark
oak trees standing tall inside a bowl-like depression among the forested hills.
North of here, the trees thin out a bit against what looks like a fortress-like
hillock. Travel is possible to the south, where a steep incline leads up onto a
wooded hill. The air here is stuffy, and the undergrowth makes it hard to see
very far in any direction. Westwards, a mossy, funnel-like rock formation allows
passage further into the dark woods.
name: Deep, Dark Wooded Gully

desc:
The top of this mountain seems to be little more than a great mound of gravel. A
few boulders, covered in viridescent lichen, emerge from the pile, adding hints
of colour to a drab grey and brown landscape. Precipitous cliffs border the
mountain on three sides, but a boulder field on the western slope seems to be a
viable, if difficult, route of descent. A jagged line of peaks continues to the
northeast, separating a wide valley to the north from a much narrower valley to
the south.
name: Barren Summit

desc:
A great host of stout beeches stretches across the level foundation of the
valley, halted only by a steep escarpment that rears up to the north. This cliff
overhangs slightly, a cresting wave ready to crash. The forest is immune to this
impression of impending doom; bramble and briar tangle to cast their leaves
under rare gaps in the cover of foliage.
name: Shadows beneath an Escarpment

desc:
Three branches of a valley meet in the shadow of a high escarpment. South and
west, the valleys are crowded with beeches and the ends of the valleys beyond
sight. To the east, stout oaks pack a deep, narrow valley which runs beneath a
high crag and into the shadows of a high plateau.
name: Branching Valley

desc:
Broad-trunked beech trees fill a deep, narrow valley. Their dense outstretched
branches tangle as they grasp at the sky. A jagged overhang is visible through
temporary openings in the obscuring foliage. The oppressive density of the
forest conceals the physical support of this massive crag.
name: Forested Valley beneath a Crag

desc:
A valley stretching away to the west comes to an end at a U-shaped set of steep
walls which surround a sudden dead-end. These walls preclude any vision of the
heights above. A few crevices and niches provide shelter for ragged nests. The
deep shade of the dead-end affords little means for the growth of vegetation
larger than stunted shrubs.
name: Shadowed Dead-End

desc:
A broad, forested ledge runs between sheer walls to either side, rising gently
to the north until it comes to a sudden halt at a dropoff more than fifty feet
high. Little can be seen of what lies at the top of either of the steep cliffs
that line the ledge. However, though the depths beneath the ledge lie in deep
gloom, the skeletal remains of rotting trees are visible, reaching branches
half-sunk in a muddy swamp.
name: Rising Forest

desc:
This region of the valley is covered in tall beeches whose high branches mask
the sky. Far beneath this dark canopy, a soggy mat of fallen leaves and broken
branches blankets the floor. Patches of luminescent mould grow on this fertile
carpet, emitting a soft eerie glow. Eastward the valley descends into a dank
morass cast in a deep gloom.
name: Forested Valley

desc:
Green, springy creepers meld with oozing mud to drag at every step through a
sodden morass in the centre of this valley. A dank, earthy odour permeates the
air, tinged with a hint of rot and decay. The thick sludge of the forest floor
appears to have suffocated all plant life except for hulking beeches and the
vines that cling to them.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
Swarms of dead insects float on pitch-black water pooling in the depressions
between thick-girthed roots. Putrid mould blackens entire tree boles, stretching
upward from the foul water which seems to be its source. The air is suffocating,
thick with a noxious vapour which evaporates from the pools and mingles with an
oppressive stench of decay.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
A thick sludge of rainwater, decaying fungi, and mouldy vegetation spreads over
the forest floor. Stunted beeches struggle to grow, their roots engulfed by the
viscous filth. Rotting creepers reach upward to latch onto writhing branches,
dragging them down to the ravenous morass.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
A massive tree has fallen here, its death throes bringing down several of its
smaller brethren. Their rotting stumps are covered in patches of black mould and
vivid orange fungi. Large, grey beetles bore into the soft, decomposing bark to
feed on the wood at the core; they are surrounded by swarms of maggots which
devour the fungi at the surface. The ground beneath the trunk is damp and
marshy; it sags beneath the great weight of the dead tree.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
A stream of water trickles over the edge of an opening high up a cliff to the
east. Its presence is obscured by a dense curtain of vines, and camouflaged by a
vibrant emerald moss that surrounds it. The water flows down leafy beech
branches stretching toward the cliff; it eventually meets the ground where it
mixes with decaying vegetation to form a viscid muck.
name: Sludge beneath a Cliff

desc:
Viscous mud drips from a looming overhang to the east. The rest of the sky is
blocked out by the thickly woven foliage of beeches that sprawl across the
valley. The leaves closest to the cliff are spattered with mud, as are various
boulders and rocks near the cliff. The ground is marshy and covered in a soft,
thick layer of mould.
name: Sludge beneath a Cliff

desc:
A plateau hangs over the valley from the east. Emerald moss stretches up the
steep face, nourished by water that seeps from cracks in a cliff wall. As this
water reaches the forest floor, it mixes with a thick, damp sludge composed of
decaying vegetation. Broad trunks of beech trees rise from this morass, their
distant crowns casting dark shadows.
name: Sludge beneath a Cliff

desc:
Heavy clouds of spores hang in the air around vivid fungi that line
broad-girthed beeches. The fruiting bodies exhibit various shades of green,
orange and red, visible wherever light peeks through the distant, obscuring
canopy. The damp air causes the spores to stick together in clearly visible
clumps, making it difficult to breathe.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
The valley narrows suddenly as a rugged mountain juts into it from the north.
Birches blanketing the slope meet densely packed beeches that dominate the
valley floor. The beeches continue to thicken to the east, and headway in that
direction is only possible by following the northern slope of the valley.
name: Narrowing Valley

desc:
The beech and birch trees on the northern slope of the valley are stricken with
disease; their bores ooze amber sap. Fragile and rotten vines hang from drooping
branches. The sodden decaying vegetation on the incline provides little
purchase. The few patches of exposed rock are little better, as they are covered
in slippery, grey-green lichen.
name: Blighted Slope

desc:
The beeches in this valley lack most of their leaves, and large swaths of bark
are missing from their trunks. Soft, rotten remains of beech-bark lie on the
forest floor atop mounds of leaves, providing sustenance to a rampant ochre
fungus. The patchy canopy lets in more light than is typical for the murky
forest, revealing the cliffs that border the valley to the north and south.
name: Rotting Trees

desc:
Ochre mould blankets trunk, roots, and limbs of sickly beeches. No bark is
visible in the rare gaps devoid of fungi. The bare, drooping branches afford a
clear view of the steep valley walls to the north and south which extend for
several miles.
name: Rotting Trees

desc:
These beeches are dead, and have been for a long time. Leafless branches droop
from their trunks. The floor is a sodden mass of decomposing vegetation covered
in a thick layer of mould and fungus. Scattered squat and sickly shrubs struggle
to survive in the septic soil.
name: Rotting Trees

desc:
Countless rows of thick grey trunks extend beyond sight in the darkness beneath
the great canopy of Mirkwood. From each trunk sprouts twisted, bulbous roots
that intertwine with one another to form a hard, knotted floor that rises as it
approaches each tree and then falls again. Rigid boughs grow together from tree
to tree to form a confusing maze in the smothering lightless environment that
the boughs themselves help to create.
name: Forested Valley

desc:
A treacherous forested slope rises to the west beneath an impenetrable canopy of
interwoven fir branches. The slope continues northward toward two towering stone
spires, leveling off into a valley toward the east. The ground is rocky with
sparse outcroppings of larger boulders wrapped in exposed tree roots.
name: Steep Wooded Slope

desc:
Disintegrating trees lie strewn across a steep slope which climbs to the south.
The slope becomes steeper and steeper until it rises vertically to the south and
becomes impassable. The slope is hemmed in by grey rock on either side and the
surrounding rock walls offer little opportunity for breeze or wind to disturb
the heavy odour of decay. The ground is soft and covered in mould, and there
seems to be nothing in the way of healthy vegetation; all has turned to rot.
name: Rotting Incline

desc:
Bulbous toadstools blanket the forest floor, thriving silently in the darkness
imposed by a thickly-woven canopy. Broken pieces ground into the earth betray
where creatures have traversed this region of the forest. The fungi sit on an
ample bed of decaying twigs and leaves mixed with sticky mud.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
Dark green and black-speckled algae cover several small murky pools of water,
contrasted by a soft and damp forest floor, which is littered by splintered
pieces of wood and dead leaves. Beeches, stunted but with thick mouldy-covered
foliage grow on the drier spots of land. Wiry and gnarly roots stick out in
clumps in various spots, forming both sturdier footholds and barriers.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
Long, narrow ridges protrude from a high escarpment to the west. Grey-green
lichen blankets these large, exposed rock faces. Stands of beeches thrive in the
narrow ravines between these ridges. Their stretching foliage obscures the
forest floor, reaching up to the height of the ridges. Gusts of wind send waves
through the canopy, making these ridges appear as if they were sandbars
breaching the surface of a rolling river.
name: Protruding Ridges

desc:
Tall, dark cliff faces hang over this narrow valley, casting shadows over the
shifting trees. The rocky walls are covered with deep, narrow crevices lost in
darkness. The only break in the trees is an old streambed that has been nearly
swallowed by overhanging vines.
name: Narrow Valley

desc:
This narrow valley is covered densely in oak trees. Patches of stones mark a dry
streambed which forms a natural path east and west. Several large boulders sit
beside the streambed; shattered trees and a track of debris show the path of
their descent from the southern slope of the valley.
name: Narrow Valley

desc:
A dry streambed curves around a peak that juts sharply from the northwest. The
oaks that populate this stretch of the valley have been considerably thinned.
Their broad stumps still leak sap, indicating that they have recently been cut.
Some of the living trees exhibit severe gashes inflicted during failed attempts
to fell them.
name: Bend in the Valley

desc:
This part of the valley is filled with the stumps of oaks, the only survivors
being scattered saplings. Rock formations on the northern mountainside exhibit
the remains of a waterfall. The rocky bed of a dry stream leads away from this
waterfall, and follows the valley to the west.
name: By a Gate in a Narrow Valley

desc:
Tall cliffs dominate this corner of a murky valley. Debris from ages of erosion
gathers in mounds at the foot of the escarpment. Beeches and oaks compete for
territory, even breaking through fallen boulders in their relentless quest for
expansion. Their branches, too, are locked in combat, tangling and intertwining.
name: Shadows beneath an Escarpment

desc:
Two great stone spires tower up through the sparse canopy on the western and
eastern sides of a lightly wooded slope, reaching far above the forest floor
resting between them. Massive moss-covered trunks have burst through the cold
hard stone between the great pillars, their lichen-covered branches growing into
one another to form a thick layer of tangled vegetation that covers many parts
of the steep and rugged terrain near the ground.
name: Two Stone Spires

desc:
A rugged ridge curves from south to east, looming over a level valley which hugs
the steeply slanting slope. The bottom of the valley is filled with stout oaks
whose branches seem to rake at the slopes of the ridge. Little grows beneath the
cover of the branches which extend several dozen feet up the slope of the ridge.
However, a few scraggly shrubs take refuge higher up the slope, out of reach of
the trees.
name: Foot of a Ridge

desc:
A broad, rounded ridge extends from a towering peak to the east. Massive oak
boles stand like stout pillars, supporting a tangled canopy dozens of yards
above their bases. These behemoths have choked out most other life below their
distant crowns. Hulking moss-covered boulders with blankets of leaves are all
that populate the shadows between the trees.
name: Shadowed Ridge

desc:
A broad ridgeline reaches out from a looming peak in the northeast, abutting
with a twisting promontory that curves around a hollow valley. These landforms
are separated by a jagged spine that divides oak-clad slopes to the north from
lindens in the south. Twisted roots attack the rocks, and crumbled remains of
boulders to either side show that the forest will ultimately prevail.
name: Shadowed Ridge

desc:
A shadowed plateau looms over a gloomy forest. Lindens tower high above the
ground, their massive crowns blocking all light. The boles of the trees are
twisted and scarred with gnarled knots accumulated through many years of growth.
Mossy roots grip the hard, barren ground between trunks. The elevated shelf
culminates in a promontory to the east and broadens into undulating hills to the
northwest.
name: Twisted Promontory

desc:
A tall bluff rises from a valley that envelops it to the east. Ancient linden
trees stretch over the encircling cliffs, knotted boles exposed to the open air.
Their gnarled trunks are swathed in dark green moss, and twisted roots protrude
through the cliff walls to hang over the shadowy murk of the valley below.
name: Twisted Promontory

desc:
Tall, black oak trees reach across this naturally occurring aisle, forming an
almost opaque surface above. To the north and south, steep walls of talus and
snarly vegetation make further passage possible only along the V-shaped
east-westerly trench. Some moisture can be found along the bottom of the V, but
there is not enough of it to use as a drinking source.
name: Wooded Corridor

desc:
A hard-packed, sandy corridor is the only traversable part of this otherwise
wild and vine-infested forest. The corridor is shaped like an 'L', and travel is
possible to the east and the south. The easterly direction leads to a deeply
wooded area, while to the south there seem to be more hills and boulders.
name: L-shaped Trench

desc:
This deeply forested mountainside is covered in a veritable jungle of vines,
thorny bushes and other creepers and plants. To the west, a stand of oaks and
vines form an impenetrable wall of vegetation, where passage is made impossible
thanks to the plethora of clinging and hanging vines and webs that have formed a
thick, spongy mat of interwoven, half-rotten foliage. Passage is possible to the
north, along a sandy path-like groove, and to the south, up onto a wooded slope.
name: Creepers and Vines

desc:
The ground slopes dramatically, down towards the southwest and up to a
precipitous peak to the northwest. The ground is dotted with heavy granite
boulders, and a great number of tall conifers grows in their midst. Travel here
is hard but not impossible, and the air feels comparatively less stuffy if not
almost fresh.
name: Precarious Bend

desc:
Musty, almost harsh fumes of decay hang in the air like a bad presence. The
rugged and uneven ground is covered in unhealthy-looking moss and lichen that
flocks around the trunks of the tall, thick old oaks that grow among a handful
of black granite boulders. Travelling is arduous here, and the only viable
routes are downslope to the south, or further along the ridge-like boulders that
can be spied between the tree trunks to the east.
name: Musty-smelling, Wooded Terrain

desc:
The trees that grow on this boulder-strewn landscape are immensely tall, and
their crowns have grown together to form an impenetrable canopy above. Another,
taller plateau can be found north of here. More forested boulders lie to the
east, and southwards it is possible to descend into a dark, wooded gully.
name: Densely Wooded Mountains

desc:
Black, sharp-edged granite boulders stand close together here, some of which are
completely overgrown with moss and other vegetation. Tall and imposing beeches
and oaks grow between the boulders, and their mighty canopies block out every
last trace of the sky above. The ground slopes down towards the south and
sharply upwards to the north, and the terrain in general is very hard to
navigate.
name: Huge Black Boulders

desc:
Dark and gnarly old oaks stand close together here, blocking out the sky. The
ground is covered by a thick mat of some sort of black, foul-smelling moss.
Looking north, one can see a big field of black boulders in between the trees,
and the ground slopes distinctly upwards.
name: Dark Forest beneath a Cliff Wall

desc:
A rugged line of mountains bulges from the forest floor, looming over the
surrounding hills. Crooked linden trees stretch out from the narrow apex to
extend their gnarled branches over sudden plunges to the north and south. Dense
foliage and vines hang from these branches, forming an obscuring curtain which
blots out the horizon. The rocky soil bears no underbrush to hold it in place,
thus providing poor purchase at the precipices.
name: Rugged Range of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
This forsaken spot is almost completely surrounded by thick, black oak trees
that grow extremely close together. To the north, passage is further hindered by
a tall wall of granite boulders, completely overgrown with spiny bushes and
gnarly roots. A big, dramatically sloping hill rises out of the forest to the
west, a climbing challenge far too difficult to contemplate even for the most
skilled of mountaineers.
name: Dead End

desc:
The trees growing here are old, sprawling oaks that have watched over these
lands since the days forest elves made their homes in the Emyn-nu-Fuin. A steep
cliff blocks the way to the west, but it looks like it is possible to climb up
with some skill. The forest grows dense in all directions, and visibility is
very limited.
name: Forest below a Ledge

desc:
The ground rises noticeably to the north while the forest slowly transforms from
a wide variety of species at the lower elevations to old, gnarled oaks higher
up. It is very dark beneath the canopy and the forest is enveloped in an
oppressive aura. To the north, a high cliff is barely visible through the
foliage.
name: Long, Forested Slope

desc:
This winding, almost stair-like path traverses a landscape comprised of wooded
granite cliffs and boulders, where tall oaks and rugged pines compete for scant
patches of fertile soil. To the north, it is possible to descend onto a lower
hill. Westwards one can spy a jagged, path-like route through the rocky terrain.
name: Sheltered Nook on a Wooded Mountainside

desc:
While this stony ridge is not completely void of trees and bushes, compared to
the surroundings it is barren and lifeless. The ground is comprised of talus and
big granite boulders. A dark-orange sort of lichen grows in wild and odd
patterns on the rocks, and if one only glances at them one can be led to think
that the patterns depict twisted people and animals.
name: Talus and Morasses

desc:
Few trees grow here on this nourishment-deprived, gravelly slope deep inside the
Mirkwood. The ones that do are of a shrubby kind, a type of black oak that grows
slowly and in strange, knotty patterns. Patches of hardy, spiny weeds grow
around the base of the trees, making it hard to approach them without wearing
some sort of armour. Passage is possible to the north and to the west.
name: Gravelly Slope

desc:
Dark, ominous-looking oak trees grow tightly together on this steep and rocky
hill. Thick, tough vines and shrubs also call this place home, and a smattering
of cobwebs among them spin a tale of the possibility of some eight-legged
inhabitants too. East of here, the forest opens up to a gravelly hilltop, and to
the south it is possible to journey downslope to an area with dead or dying oak
trees. The hills and the trees conspire to make it impossible to go in any other
direction.
name: Mountain Forest

desc:
The ground here is exceptionally dry, and cracks can been seen in the surface of
the earth. Most of the trees here are thin and wasted, allowing for plant-life,
such as scrub growth and stunted shrubs, to grow instead. It seems that they
have been cut off from their water supply; probably as a result of the massive
landslide that can be seen on the western edge of the trees.
name: Rocky Forest

desc:
Trees grow very closely here, all connected by their interweaving branches.
Small stones lie scattered across the forest floor, almost completely covered by
moss. Mottled unhealthy-looking lichen and sickly, green moss grow on almost
everything, and the air has an inky, bitter odour.
name: Musty Forest

desc:
A number of impressive trunks stand here forming a ring around what appears to
be a small pool of water. The trees, although numerous, do not grow closely
together but stand with what appears as an almost intentional space between
them. A few hints of sky can be glimpsed through the canopy above. These great
trees grow right down to the water's edge, with the water almost lapping against
their roots.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
Huge oak and beech trees grow all over this rocky area. A steep cliff blocks the
way to the east and north, but it might be possible to climb up the cliff to
reach a ledge. The faint light that might otherwise penetrate the canopy is
completely blocked out by the tall, steep cliffs. To the south, the forest
foliage is slightly less dense and can be navigated.
name: Forest below a Ledge

desc:
Towering trees grow in small, dense thickets that look almost identical to one
another. In between the groves of trees, thick shrubbery thrives. The bushes are
tall and thorny and the paths running between them are narrow and dark. The
undergrowth rustles with movement from rodents and other small wildlife.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
The still, dank air makes breathing difficult and adds a distinct nuance to the
area that sets it apart from an ordinary forest. The source of the odour appears
to be stands of decaying trees that are scattered throughout the area. A faint
trickling sound is ever present even though there appears to be no creak or
stream nearby.
name: Rotting Trees

desc:
There is a huge, vertical cliff to the north and west. In the shades of the
cliff, the trees do not grow as high as in the deeper parts of the forest.
Passage to the north and west is blocked by a vertical cliff, and there are many
small rocks and scree at the foot of the cliff. The shaded cliff is moist with
water, and some fallen leaves have gotten caught by the wetness and now stick to
the side of the near-vertical cliff.
name: Forest under a Cliff

desc:
There is a strong musk emanating from somewhere deeper in the forest. The cliff
to the north of the forest is rising almost vertically up, and there is no way
to climb it here. There are strong markings of animal paws and claws on the
ground. Next to the cliff, there is a mound of soil and dirt.
name: Musky Forest

desc:
The entrance to this underground den is dug below the roots of a huge fir tree.
The den itself is dug mostly below a cliff; the space is big enough for a pack
of beasts and high enough for a man to stand upright. The floor and walls to
either side of the entrance consist of dense soil while the back wall is rock.
The air is dusty and the smell is musty inside the den.
name: Hideous Den

desc:
There were once trees here in this mountainous wood, but they have been
completely overgrown by strangling vines, creepers and roots. Together, the
vegetation forms a dense, spiny mat that chokes off both light and possible
directions of travel. The air is stale and thick with rotting fumes - both from
decomposing leaves and something else, more sinister.
name: Strangling Vegetation

desc:
The ground, as far as it can be seen amongst the dark, towering pines, is sandy
and partially covered with needles and moss. South of here, the forest floor
rises up considerably, forming a high mountainous wall of rocks, brambles and
squat trees. A few dark shapes are standing in amongst the trees. They look like
lurking, hulking shadows, but upon closer inspection it is clear that they are
merely boulders covered with tortorous roots and branches.
name: Dark Forest Floor

desc:
Huge, dark fir trees stand closely spaced apart here in this shrub-filled and
quiet part of the great Mirkwood. Shrubs and boulders make it impossible to
travel in any other directions than south and east, but at least it is not hard
to journey in those directions. Two trees have been chopped down here at some
point in the not too distant past, and they are accompanied by the remnants of a
small, abandoned campfire.
name: Still Forest

desc:
The dominant species of tree here is a huge, dark fir that grows to several
hundred feet in height. The trees have great, needle-rich crowns that form a
dense, almost impenetrable canopy above. Below the canopy, the trunks of the
trees are bare, leaving no place for birds to sit or for moss to grow. The
general roughness of terrain conspires to make further travel possible only to
the east and west.
name: Towering Firs

desc:
Huge fir trees stand close together with black oaks in this dark and foreboding
forest. The ground is littered with leaves and pine needles, and a rich flora of
moss and lichen grow among piles of small, round rocks and talus. This type of
terrain is rough, and further travel is only possible to the west and to the
north from here.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
A cairn comprised of mossy rocks and boulders is barely discernible among the
abundant trees. There are no signs that the cairn fills any purpose, because it
appears utterly abandoned and never used. The thick, bushy forest continues in
all directions save west, where tangled shrubs and boulders make it impossible
to find a viable path.
name: Cairn in the Forest

desc:
The air here is still and unmoving, probably because of the many, many fallen
trees and rotting logs that languish on the uneven, sloping forest floor. The
only accessible part of this whole area is a small trench that leads west into a
fresher part of the forest, and south up onto some sort of wooded hill.
name: Stale Forest

desc:
The only thing that gives away the fact that this is a hill is the curvature of
the forest floor, because the many trees and the thick underbrush makes it hard
to see much of anything here. The only way to go from here is north - all other
directions are either blocked by impassable obstacles, or the ground slopes
downward so severely that not even the most skilled climber would risk the
descent.
name: Wooded Hill

desc:
The rugged mix of oak shrub and conifers that grow here is challenged by the
vines and shrubs that threaten to suffocate them with their tendrils and roots.
East of here, the forest floor slopes upward onto some sort of hill. Travel is
also possible to the south and west, further into the depths of Mirkwood.
name: Rugged Forest

desc:
These trees once grew to mighty heights, but many years ago they were blighted
by a disease or poison of some kind. Now, only their trunks and main branches
remain, whitened by age. Curiously enough, there is no rot in the trunks, and
one may wonder if there was some sort of unnatural cause to their demise.
name: Patch of Dead Trees

desc:
The trees close ranks here, making it impossible to go anywhere but north. The
tree trunks stand incredibly close together, and all the space in between them
are taken up by bushes, vines and other overgrowth. A few rotten strands of
spider webs hang suspended from the tree branches. The webs look old and
withered, almost flimsy.
name: Impassable Forest

desc:
The ground slopes dramatically here, up from the dark forest floor below and
onto a high plateau-like shelf. There are plenty of trees here, mostly conifers
but also a handful of dark, old gnarled beeches. Here and there among the roots
of the trees and bushes one can discern both talus and larger granite boulders.
name: Steep Incline

desc:
Aside from the thick, dark roots that criss-cross this confined space, there are
hardly any noteworthy features here. Black, rotting leaves and flaky dirt make
up the floor, walls and most of the ceiling. The smell in here is foul, a
slow-rotting scent of vegetable decay is coupled with something more sinister,
that of a sick, dying animal.
name: Underneath the Treestump

desc:
A group of dark, black-trunked oak trees grow close together here. The ground
beneath them is covered by a thick layer of rotting undergrowth and smelly old
leaves. A handful of the trees died a long time ago, but because they stood so
close together they have been unable to fall down properly. Now they are rotting
in the places where they once grew and prospered, bearing an eerie resemblance
to hollowed-out, cavity-filled teeth standing among a row of comparatively
healthy ones.
name: Dark Grove of Black Oaks

desc:
Thick, spiny brambles and dead foliage cover the bottom of this horse-shoe
shaped depression in the forest. Naked granite walls are visible through the
cascade of dead and dying roots and vines that hang down from the trees that
grow along the edges of the trough. Travel is impossible in every direction
except the east, where a grove of tall, dark oak trees eke out a tortured and
light-starved existence.
name: Trough-like Hollow

desc:
