A swift, dark stream carves a rocky channel through this region of Mirkwood. The
land rises to the east and south, and becomes mountainous. The stream flows
north into an area full of rocky outcroppings and canyons. A swarm of maggots
feeds upon the mangled remains of a large mammal that rests by the stream.
Black, gnarled oaks crowd the western bank, a looming horde held back only by
the gurgling water.
name: Murky Tributary of the Gulduin

desc:
A dark, rocky brook runs through the forested mountains, opaque water clouding
its bed. Livid colonies of mould infest wilting lilies that float on the clouded
water. Torpid mosquito swarms fill the air with fitful buzzing as they hover
listlessly over the stream. Grey moss-clad cliffs rise throughout the forest,
obstructing the stream's path and forcing it to detour around them.
name: Murky Tributary of the Gulduin

desc:
A dark, clouded stream cuts a sharp bend around a rocky outcrop, disappearing
down a narrow canyon to the west. A profound silence reigns among the oaks
flanking the waterway. Great boughs, laden with leaves in shades of green and
brown, overshadow the stream and further darken the already murky water.
name: Bend in a Tributary of the Gulduin

desc:
A murky stream cascades from a waterfall to the east and flows down a rocky
slope. Its colour morphs as it runs: black-speckled leaves mingle with withered
twigs on their way downstream. The forest near the stream is unnaturally still,
as if lulled into a torpor by the murmuring flow.
name: Murky Tributary of the Gulduin

desc:
A dark cliff looms over a line of hills, casting perpetual shadows over their
rocky summits. Sharp, protruding boulders hang precariously from these heights,
as if poised to pounce on some unseen prey. A sea of trees drowns the valleys
between these hills and sways under passing breezes.
name: Jagged Foothills

desc:
Rocky outcrops watch silently over a dark green sea of oaks whose branches reach
for these heights as if to drag them down. Distant summits soar above the forest
to the east, heralds of formidable mountains whose foundations lie hidden beyond
the trees. The face of a promontory looms just to the north; dark recesses in
its cliff wall provide natural lairs for unknown creatures.
name: Jagged Foothills

desc:
A tall crest of rock rises to the south, its narrow ridges reaching as if to
grasp at these broken foothills. The forest seems to flee from the heights;
stunted saplings and scrawny shrubs are all that persist here. Jet-black moss
obscures a field of massive boulders that chokes out any vegetation that might
otherwise grow between the trees.
name: Shadows of a Ridge

desc:
Tall cliffs stretch over this narrow cleft in the mountains. A stream of slimy,
viscous water pours into a deep, dark pool which teems with ochre vegetation
whose protruding flowers exude a rank stench. Small insects with long, spindly
legs skim across the surface of the pool.
name: Noxious Pool

desc:
A fist of rock stretches over this shadowed depression. Its broken underside is
covered in skittering masses of creatures whose chittering fills the forest.
Pungent fungi thrive in the shadows of the crag, spreading over a dank, rotten
bed of vegetation that swallows any footsteps that may fall upon it.
name: Beneath a Crag

desc:
The surrounding sea of oaks is displaced by a stand of alders anchored to a
craggy mountainside that rises to the north and east. Dark foliage obscures the
sky, casting a pall over the mottled trunks of the trees. Fleshy fungi spread
over the forest floor, devouring a rotting layer of vegetation. The foetid
spores of these fungi form choking clouds that hover in the air.
name: Incursion of Alders

desc:
These hills gaze out over gloomy shadows that inhabit the valleys below.
Creeping vines have consumed this highland, swallowing tree and rock alike.
Fungi envelop a massive rotten oak that exudes thick, viscous sap. The
foreboding peaks of the Emyn-nu-Fuin loom to the northeast.
name: Foothills of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
This low, barren peak rises like an island from the murky shadows of the forest.
The forest appears ready to devour it. The rocky boulders at the peak's crown
have held off the trees, but dark vines crawl through every gap and crevice.
Sharp slopes nearly surround this isolated highland, the only escape lying to
the west in the form of a narrow ridgeline that connects this hill to the next.
name: Barren Promontory

desc:
Thick clods of black dirt form the walls and ceiling of this cave, supported by
a tangle of thick, gnarled roots. The ground is damp and covered in muddy pools.
The only dry spot is a massive slab of rock in the deepest part of the cave. A
thick layer of fur, leaves, and bark blankets this slab.
name: Root-lined Cave

desc:
A stand of firs grows on a level plateau lying in the shade of a mighty peak to
the north. Sheer escarpments on every other side have isolated this alpine
biome. A foul spring issues from a cleft in the mountainside, pouring into a
dark pool that absorbs all light. Withered branches droop into the pool from
trees near the water. The pool drains via a stream which trickles over mossy
boulders before cascading over a drop-off to the west.
name: Murky Spring

desc:
This mountaintop crowns a ridge that runs northeast from here. Bounded by steep
approaches on all sides, this level summit might at first appear to be an
isolated shelter from the dangers of Mirkwood. However, frequent gusts of wind
seem insistent in their attempts to cast any hapless climber back down to the
shadows below.
name: Windswept Mountaintop

desc:
An elongated mound of raised terrain connects a high ridgeline to the south with
the comparatively lower elevation of a mountain range that lies to the north.
Sombre alders occupy the relatively level apex of the mound, choking out any
other vegetation that tries to grow there. However, the steep exposed rocks on
the slopes of this ridge do afford modest growth in the form of moss, lichen,
and scraggly shrubs.
name: Crest of a Ridge

desc:
A spiny ridgeline looms above the vastness of Mirkwood. Vertebral boulders
protrude from its rim, and claw-like crags reach out into the murky, seething
forest to either side. A towering mountain rears up to the southwest, its
tooth-like peak gnawing at the sky.
name: Crest of a Ridge

desc:
This narrow ridge is flanked by steep, yawning cliffs. These sheer faces stretch
over the mottled shadows of the valleys below. Creeping vines envelop the rocky
crest, their crimson buds issuing a feculent stench. Carrion birds perch on
scattered boulders, feasting on putrid flesh.
name: Crest of a Ridge

desc:
Unstable scree slopes line this narrow ridge, fending off the press of the
forest. Nests of carrion birds are visible in crevices below, the results of
their scrounging revealed in scattered mounds of bones. Precarious boulders
crown these heights, providing fragile purchase for creatures who wish to travel
in any direction but down. A formidable peak rises to the west.
name: Crest of a Ridge

desc:
Thick shrubs and stunted trees make up the immediate surroundings. However,
while the forest is light, the undergrowth is very dense and the thin branches
of the bushes thriving here have very long, sharp and black thorns. Especially
to the west, branches, shrubs, and thorns are intertwined together, making it
impossible to travel in that direction. The forest seems to grow thicker to the
north and south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
There is a jagged fissure at the pinnacle of this hill, and at the bottom a cave
or tunnel can be seen. This steep, rounded hill is surprisingly smooth, clad in
green moss and very slippery. It is possible to slide down the hill's sides but
climbing back up might be impossible. The Old Forest Road is visible to the
north at the foot of the hill.
name: Steep Slope above the Old Forest Road

desc:
Towering oaks stretch over this patch of forest-clad hills, obscuring the sky
with their opaque canopy. Thick branches droop to the ground, weighed down by
heavy blankets of vines. Blind ravines cross this choked woodland, their steep,
sudden slopes inviting a careless plummet. The air is humid with moisture from
the silent respiration of the trees.
name: Shadowed Valley

desc:
This narrow valley is bordered by tree-laden slopes. A cloudy brook oozes over
moss-covered stones, its pitch-black mire carrying debris from decaying
vegetation upstream. The persistent hum of insects pervades this dank gorge.
name: Dark Ravine

desc:
A cold wind rushes across the hills as if running away from a mountain peak in
the west. The trees seem to be on a mission of covering all available ground
with growth. Dark oaks and thick maples spring up at every opportunity, sending
thick roots twisting and swirling in and out of the exposed, rocky soil. The
foliage rustles constantly in the breeze.
name: Windy Hills

desc:
Punching out of the surrounding hills, this mountain is a baby - by mountain
standards. Far in the north, a taller peak looms while in the far northeast, an
enormous mountain range dominates the skyline. In all directions, forested hills
and dark woods spread out like an earth-tone quilt made out of black, green, and
brown patches.
name: Small Peak

desc:
The hills here are lumpy and bumpy, sometimes rising and sometimes falling as if
they can't make their minds up to become mountains or not. Weighing them down is
a large overgrowth of dark trees, thick brush, and gnarled roots. The dense
undergrowth is mirrored by a thick, green canopy above.
name: Hills of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The ground here rises above the surrounding Mirkwood as it snuggles up to a
small peak to the east. Festooned with gnarly brown oaks and strewn with thick
roots, the land is not easy to traverse. The underbrush is dense with thorny
plants of all types that seem to delight in catching on clothes and skin.
name: Gnarly Hill

desc:
The rocks and trees seem to be competing for dominance in this area. On every
rising rocky hill, dozens of dark oaks perch like flocks of vultures. The trees
cast long shadows during the day and obscure the moonlight at night. For that
reason, the way is dim and treacherous for foot and hoof alike.
name: Stony Hillocks

desc:
The landscape here is blasted and lifeless. The rocky hills are covered with the
corpses of trees that look like old men who died standing up while holding each
other. The once-verdant foliage, although dead, is nevertheless thick enough to
keep out most sunlight, making the area dim.
name: Dead Hills

desc:
Dark trees march across the rising landscape like squads of black troops.
Gnarled and weathered by time, hoary oaks seem to huddle together blocking out
most of the sunlight overhead as the undergrowth crunches drily with each step.
The hills rise and leap as if competing with each other to see who can go
higher.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
Though sometimes rising above the treeline proper, these hills have still been
overtaken by clumps of dark trees and bushes that seem to have formed social
cliques. The land rises and falls sharply and the way is littered with snaggly
roots and shifting loose rock.
name: Tall Hills

desc:
Conquered by trees and bushes, these hills nevertheless stand tall as they slope
up and down in waves. The forest canopy seems intent on keeping sunlight from
ever reaching the ground as a network of interwoven branches provide continual
shade. Small rodents nervously squeak here and there and birds seem to flit from
tree to tree as if uncomfortable on their perches.
name: Shaded Hills

desc:
The landscape, in shape of a gentle slope, is filled with towering black barked
oaks blocking out the sky. The slope slants into a forest to the east, while
hills dominate the horizon to the north, west and south. A black and white moss
covers the boulder-strewn slope, and foul smelling fungi thrive in the shadows
cast by the larger stones.
name: Tangled Slope

desc:
Rows of ancient, gnarled oaks and firs line a boulder-strewn ridge. There are
wide gaps in the canopy above rocky outcroppings and the largest boulders. The
undergrowth, mostly small bushes, grow in clusters around these rocks and
boulders, where the openings in the canopy are like an oasis from the eternal
gloom of Mirkwood.
name: Tree-lined Ridge

desc:
Trees with dark brown bark grow in sparsely spread rows, the gaps between them
filled with various berry bushes and thorny briars. Black moss covers much of
the trees' trunks, and a type of bone-white lichen, which gently swings whenever
a breeze sifts through Mirkwood, hangs from the gnarly branches.
name: Dusky Copse

desc:
A hill rises up amongst the forest, covered in thorny briar patches and spindly
bushes. Some oak trees grow scattered across the knoll, with bone-white, thorny
vines dangling from their gnarled limbs. Although the forest is sparse, the
crowns of these ancient giants reach dozens of feet in all directions, as if
reaching for one another.
name: Thorny Bluff

desc:
A dense wall of trees rings a foetid pond that occupies the lowest depths of a
broad valley. The water of the pool absorbs all light, guarding the secret of
what lies beneath its surface. Several barren trunks of long-dead trees stand in
the midst of the toxic water. The roots nearest to the pond blacken and shrivel
as they carry poison back to their dying trees. A muddy slope to the west feeds
the pond with black, sticky mud.
name: Mirkwood Morass

desc:
The thickly growing trees and shrubs around this small enclosed space create a
perfect hiding place inside the shrubbery. The growth of vines, brush, and trees
is so thick that it blocks all directions except back to the east through the
shrubs. A road is visible to the south behind the shrubs and trees.
name: Hidden Shrubbery

desc:
This narrow tunnel runs a few feet beneath the forest floor of Mirkwood. Thick
roots poke through the roof, making the low tunnel even lower. A small stream of
black water crosses the tunnel at its lowest part, creating a wide pool of mud
around it. The tunnel might serve as the lair of a wild animal, as bones, some
with meat still clinging to them, litter the ground.
name: Hidden Stream

desc:
The ground seems to sag as it descends to the east, where jagged hilltops rim a
low, murky valley. Oak trees cling to the slopes, dense webs of roots mixing
with those of their neighbours. Erosion has swept away much of the top layer of
soil, exposing the undersides of the roots where swarms of insects now thrive.
name: Entrance to a Broad Valley

desc:
Giant oaks tower over the western end of a broad valley. Barrel-thick roots
snake through a thick, decaying mat of leaves and acorns, and massive limbs
block out the sky. Tough goutweed proliferates in dense patches between the
trees. An oppressive stench of rot and mould fills the air.
name: Looming Oaks

desc:
The only place upon which to stand on here is a ledge that runs around the rim
of this smelly, dark pit. The spiky, snaking roots that hang down from the rich
vegetation above also offer decent hand- and footholds. Below, one can barely
make out the tips of a great number of spear-like stakes that jut out of the
pitch-black darkness.
name: Rotten Pit

desc:
The ground in this forested, rough area forms a V-shaped gully that runs from
east to west. It is impossible to travel south, where conifers and oak shrubs
grow among talus, boulders and treacherous cliffs. Passage eastwards seems
fairly straightforward, but the gully narrows a great deal to the west, leaving
only a small, dark opening, partly covered by rotting foliage and knee-high
ferns.
name: Wooded Gully
